exemplar.title: To what extent does the Spanish public school system's teaching of colonization of Latin America reflect the ideas of Paulo Freire?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does the Spanish public school system's teaching of colonization of Latin America reflect the ideas of Paulo Freire?

strengths: Report follows a clear, logical fourâsection structure guiding the reader
Language is generally clear, concise, and academically appropriate
weaknesses: Minor lapses in citation format and a few incomplete sentences interrupt fluency
Typographical inconsistencies in the worksâcited section detract from professional polish
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Organization and clarity are strong overall, but recurring citation and typographical issues prevent a fullâband performance. A mark of 2/3 captures clear communication with room for improvement in editorial precision.

strengths: Issue of power and sovereignty is clearly identified and justified with relevant context
Engagement activities (interviews and observations) are well linked to the political issue
Importance of the project is convincingly argued with personal and theoretical reasons
weaknesses: Minor gaps in logistical detail prevent the justification from reaching full depth
Relevance of engagement could be tightened by explicitly tying all activity details back to the political issue
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments placed two elements of Explanation & Justification in band 3 and one in band 4. The issue is clearly identified, the context is well explained, and the importance is justified, but minor linkage and depth gaps keep this at the top of band 3 (3/4).

strengths: Evidence of purposeful selection of secondary sources and interviews
Logical choice of engagement activities (teacher and official interviews, observations)
weaknesses: Planning lacks clear timelines, questionâdesign criteria, and resource considerations
Integration between research findings and engagement planning is only occasional
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three Process strands scored band 2: basic research planning, patchy engagement planning, and intermittent integration of research with engagement. This consistency supports a midâband mark of 2/3.

strengths: Accurate application of core Global Politics concepts (power, Eurocentrism, banking vs. problemâposing)
Clear analysis of curricular bias and structural power dynamics
weaknesses: Analysis remains at surface to midâdepth levels, without multilayered or exhaustive exploration
Synthesis of stakeholder perspectives is uneven and could further integrate interview insights into theory
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (application of concepts, depth of analysis) sit in the 3â4 band and one (synthesis) at the lower end of that band. The student delivers adequate and mostly accurate use of concepts and balanced analysis, but lacks the insight and sustained weaving necessary for higher levels. A midâband mark of 3/8 reflects this performance.

strengths: Reflection notes clear strengths (access to interviews) and limitations (curricular rigidity)
Personal biases are acknowledged and linked to topic choice
weaknesses: Evaluation remains general rather than systematic; criteria are not fully developed
Reflective insights into future practice and deeper metacognition are limited
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each Evaluation & Reflection strand was placed in the mid band, demonstrating partial evaluation and adequate reflection. However, the lack of systematic criteria and only moderate insight into learning supports a mark at the lower end of the band, 2/6.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student explicitly names the political issue as âPower and Sovereigntyâ and immediately connects it to the Spanish history curriculum. A concise but precise definition of power is provided, and ample contextual information (Euro-centric curriculum share, omitted events, textbook bias) clarifies why this is a political issue. The clarity and relevance satisfy the top-band descriptor.

The submission shows evidence of selecting a range of secondary sources and arranging interviews, but it does not outline timelines, question design, or criteria for source selection. Planning is therefore basic rather than thorough.

Activities are chosen logically (teachers, inspector, class visits) yet the write-up lacks scheduling, risk or resource considerations. This indicates planning that is present but patchy, matching band 2.

Core GP concepts (power, Eurocentrism, banking vs problem-posing pedagogy) are explained and applied accurately to evidence from interviews and textbooks across the essay. The use is purposeful but not consistently sophisticated, placing it at the top of the adequate band (4).

Research and engagement inform each other intermittentlyâe.g., interview findings drive discussion of Freire/Giroux and classroom examplesâbut the diary does not show a sustained feedback loop. Hence âsometimes informsâ (band 2).

Both engagements (teacher/official interviews and classroom observations) are fully described and directly tied to investigating âbanking educationâ and Eurocentrism, demonstrating convincing relevance. Minor logistical detail gaps keep the mark at 3 rather than 4.
Language is consistently clear, academic and grammatically accurate, with only minor lapses (e.g., typos on âwhile maleâ). Ideas are coherent and concise, fully meeting the top-band description.

The analysis moves beyond description, comparing curricular content over time, linking interview testimony to Freire/Giroux theory, and identifying structural power dynamics. While insightful, it does not reach the âmultilayered, exhaustiveâ standard of 7-8, so 4 is appropriate.
Personal Eurocentric bias is acknowledged repeatedly and linked to topic choice; however, the impact on data interpretation is only briefly mentioned, fitting mid-band performance.

The report follows a clear four-section structure (Intro, Engagements, Analysis, Works Cited) that effectively guides the reader. Transitions are smooth, reflecting purposeful organisation worthy of full marks in this criterion.

The student notes strengths (first-hand access, rich interviews) and limitations (curricular rigidity, teacher discretion) but evaluation remains general and lacks systematic criteria. This aligns with âpartial evaluationâ at 3.

The student justifies importance through a strong personal connection (having studied the curriculum), through global-political significance (curricular Eurocentrism reproducing power), and through explicit reference to Freire/Giroux debates. While persuasive, the rationale is not exceptionally wide-ranging; a mid-top-band mark is warranted.

Perspectives of teachers, an education inspector, textbook authors and Indigenous voices are presented and compared; the student explains how they converge or diverge on Eurocentrism. Some weaving is evident but the synthesis is uneven, meriting the lower end (3) of the 3-4 band.
The reflection recognises growth in critical awareness and calls for curricular change, but does not project detailed future implications or deeper metacognitive insight. Adequate reflection = 3.

annotations.comment: The textbook bias discussion effectively links source analysis to power dynamics in teaching content.
The phrase âjustified as aâ is left hanging and needs completion for reader comprehension.
The conclusion succinctly contrasts Freire and Giroux, reinforcing the studentâs evaluative stance.
Consider adding a brief evaluation of how your personal bias shaped the conclusionâs comparative judgment.
The works cited section is comprehensive, demonstrating wide research, but citation formats vary inconsistently.
Several web sources lack uniform access-date formatting; standardize entries to improve professional communication.
The title and section header provide a clear entry point into the engagement activity, effectively guiding the reader into the investigation.
The research question is prominently displayed but could be tightened by explicitly linking its phrasing to the political issue of power and sovereignty.
The statement âPolitical Issue: Power and Sovereigntyâ is concise and precisely identifies the issue under study.
The introductory definition of power is well supported by IB Global Politics material, but its direct connection to the Spanish curriculum could be made more explicit.
The curriculum context paragraph offers strong quantitative detail (10% coverage) and multiple sources, demonstrating clear justification of importance.
The discussion of omitted events (e.g., Atahualpaâs execution) is insightful, but it would benefit from a brief linkage back to how this omission perpetuates power imbalances.
Freireâs theoretical overview is comprehensive, showing strong engagement with primary texts.
Consider adding a sentence that situates Freireâs concepts explicitly within the Spanish teaching context you investigate.
The paragraph defining problem-posing education is clear but reads in isolation; integrate a concrete example from your engagements to deepen the link.
The personal narrative about moving to Tanzania offers genuine reflection and strengthens the justification of project importance.
The Giroux discussion is thorough, but consider specifying how his critique uniquely illuminates the Spanish case beyond Freireâs lens.
The research question is restated clearly, reinforcing focus just before engagement activities.
The description of the teacher and inspector interviews is vivid and clearly linked to your political issue.
The engagement plan lacks explicit timing or schedule details, making feasibility harder to assess.
Observing actual history lessons strengthens the link between theory and practice, demonstrating purposeful engagement.
The engagement analysis section begins well, but the example description is cut off mid-sentence and needs completion.
You effectively integrate Freireâs collaboration ideal with teacher interviews, showing research informing engagement.
The synthesis of Girouxâs expansion and the inspectorâs interview is strong, but clarify how new findings reshape your research questions.
The sentence ending abruptly after âwhile male authorsâ disrupts clarity; revise to complete the thought.
The logical structure of sections AâD aids reader navigation and reflects purposeful organization.
Minor typographical lapses (e.g., âlaseâ instead of âlaserâ) appear in citations; a final proofread would eliminate these small errors.

rubricData.name: Global Politics IA: Engagement Activity
rubricData.description: For the new Global Politics syllabus

strands.name: Political issue identification & clarity
strands.content:
1. Issue not identified or irrelevant
2. No meaningful explanation

strands.name: Project importance & suitability
strands.content: No discussion of why the project matters

strands.name: Relevance of engagement activities
strands.content: Activities absent or unrelated to issue

strands.name: Political issue identification & clarity
strands.content:
1. Issue named but explanation vague / superficial
2. Limited or missing context

strands.name: Project importance & suitability
strands.content: Importance / suitability mentioned but weak or generic

strands.name: Relevance of engagement activities
strands.content: Activities listed; relevance implied or weakly linked

strands.name: Political issue identification & clarity
strands.content:
1. Issue explicitly stated
2. Clear, precise explanation with relevant context

strands.name: Project importance & suitability
strands.content: Importance / suitability clearly justified with specific reasons

strands.name: Relevance of engagement activities
strands.content: Activities fully explained and convincingly linked to the issue

strands.name: Integration of research â engagement
strands.content: No link between research and engagement

strands.name: Engagement planning
strands.content: Activities planned but timing / logistics patchy

strands.name: Integration of research â engagement
strands.content: Research consistently informs engagement; findings feed back into further inquiry

strands.name: Depth of political-issue analysis
strands.content: Clear, balanced analysis; key factors explored

strands.name: Synthesis of stakeholder perspectives
strands.content: Effective synthesis; perspectives woven into coherent argument

strands.name: Awareness of personal bias
strands.content: Biases explored; impact on work examined

strands.name: Organization & structure
strands.content: Logical structure; mostly easy to follow
